verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 is a type II membrane protein with an N-terminal TM anchor. Its primary residence is the Golgi, where it functions as a galactosyltransferase. However, it is well-documented that B4GALT1 is also expressed on the cell surface — notably on sperm and other cell types — where it acts as a receptor for zona pellucida glycoproteins and modulates signaling. This surface pool is genuine (the TM anchor remains; it is not shed), making it a dual-localization protein with a documented PM minority fraction alongside its dominant Golgi residency.
